text: Mark: Hi there, Nam! You aren't still working on your history assignment, are you?
You aren't still working on ... , are you?
translation: هنوز داری روی ... کار می‌کنی، درسته؟
explanation: ساختار پرکاربرد پرسیدن در ادامهٔ فعالیتی که انتظار داریم شاید تمام شده باشد.

text: Nam: Hi, Victor! No, I'm taking a break.
I'm taking a break
translation: دارم استراحت می‌کنم / یک وقفه گرفتم
explanation: عبارتی بسیار رایج برای گفتن اینکه فعلاً کار را متوقف کرده‌اید.

text: I'm looking at some photos, actually - a family celebration.
Do you want to see them?
explanation: پیشنهاد مؤدبانه با Do you want to...؟

text: Mark: So, who's the cute baby in this picture here?
cute baby
explanation: واژهٔ کاربردی برای توصیف نوزاد یا چیز دوست‌داشتنی.

text: Nam: She's my niece - my brother's daughter. Her name's Tae-Hee. She's one year old in this picture. It's a very important birthday in Korea -
We call it 'Dol' or Doljanchi
explanation: معرفی یک مراسم فرهنگی.

text: . It's a very special celebration - in fact, I think it's probably more important than a wedding or a graduation!
we invite all our family and friends
translation: همهٔ اعضای خانواده و دوستان را دعوت می‌کنیم
explanation: ساختاری رایج برای صحبت دربارهٔ مراسم‌ها.

text: and sometimes our neighbours. Here's a picture of the restaurant where we celebrated Tae-Hee's big day.
big day
explanation: اصطلاح پرکاربرد برای روزهای ویژه مانند تولد یا مراسم‌ها.

text: Mark: What a lovely place - and a beautiful garden, too.
What a ...!
explanation: ساختار رایج برای ابراز تعجب یا تحسین.

text: Nam: Yes, it's a perfect location for taking photographs. Can you see me just there next to the trees?
perfect location
explanation: عبارتی متداول برای توصیف مکان مناسب.

text: Mark: Ah yes, I like your hat! And I like the red and silver hat Tae-Hee's wearing too. It's really pretty.
really pretty
explanation: عبارت کاربردی برای توصیف ظاهر چیزها.

text: Nam: Yes, it's a traditional hat for a girl. Baby boys wear a different one - all black. It looks very serious!
It looks very serious
explanation: ساختار رایج It looks + adjective

text: Mark: That's a bit boring. Black and silver would be more interesting.
a bit boring
explanation: کاربرد رایج boring و تقویت‌کنندهٔ a bit

text: Nam: What's Tae-Hee wearing round her skirt?
wearing round her skirt
explanation: ساختار wearing برای توضیح لباس/اشیا روی بدن.

text: Nam: That's a little purse. Boy babies wear this too. It's made of silk and it means good luck in our culture. And she's wearing a belt too. Look - do you see? The belt means she will have a long life!
good luck
explanation: یکی از رایج‌ترین اصطلاحات در زبان انگلیسی.

text: long life
explanation: ترکیب وصفی بسیار رایج.

text: Mark: And what's this on the table there? It's very colourful.
very colourful
explanation: صفت کاربردی برای توصیف اشیای رنگی.

text: Nam: It's rice cakes.
rice cakes
explanation: غذای سنتی شرق آسیا.

text: Mark: I've never seen anything like that before.
I've never seen anything like that before
translation: تا حالا چیزی مثل این ندیده‌ام
explanation: ساختار رایج Present Perfect برای تجربه‌ها.

text: It looks like a rainbow!
looks like
explanation: ساختار ساده و بسیار پراستفاده.

text: Nam: We always eat rice cakes at a baby's party. There are usually 12 different types of them on the table. Look - this rice cake is completely orange in colour and this one here is bright green!
bright green
explanation: ترکیب bright برای رنگ‌های درخشان.

text: Mark: It looks like a vegetable!
Nam: Now, look - this is me with my brother's wife, Mi-Cha. We get on really well together.
We get on really well
translation: خیلی با هم خوب کنار می‌آییم
explanation: اصطلاح بسیار مهم برای روابط انسانی.

text: Mark: What are you holding?
Nam: Oh, it's a little bag. All the guests get a gift bag at the end of the party. And inside there's a present from the baby's parents.
gift bag
explanation: مفهومی رایج در مهمانی‌ها.

text: It's really fun to open it because you don't know what you'll get.
you don't know what you'll get
explanation: ساختار رایج برای ایجاد کنجکاوی.

text: So it could be a candle, or some chocolates. Guess what I got? You can see it in this next photo.
Guess what?
explanation: اصطلاح محاوره‌ای برای جلب توجه.

text: Mark: It looks like a box of tea. But shouldn't it be the other way round - the baby gets the presents?
the other way round
explanation: اصطلاح مهم برای گفتن اینکه ترتیب یا حالت خلاف انتظار است.

text: Nam: Well, the baby receives money from the guests, so in a way you're right! And actually, I've got my present right here. So, why don't we open the box right now and have a cup of tea? Then we can try to finish our assignments.
Why don't we ...?
explanation: پیشنهاد مؤدبانه و بسیار رایج.

text: Mark: Sounds good to me!
